root_text.sn48.49:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā kosambiyaṁ viharati ghositārāme.
root_text.sn48.49:1.3: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmatā piṇḍolabhāradvājena aññā byākatā hoti:
root_text.sn48.49:1.4: “khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyāti pajānāmī”ti.
root_text.sn48.49:1.5: Atha kho sambahulā bhikkhū yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu. Ekamantaṁ nisinnā kho te bhikkhū bhagavantaṁ etadavocuṁ:
root_text.sn48.49:2.2: ‘khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyā’ti pajānāmīti.
root_text.sn48.49:2.3: Kiṁ nu kho, bhante, atthavasaṁ sampassamānena āyasmatā piṇḍolabhāradvājena aññā byākatā:
root_text.sn48.49:2.4: ‘khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyā’ti pajānāmī”ti?
root_text.sn48.49:3.1: “Tiṇṇannaṁ kho, bhikkhave, indriyānaṁ bhāvitattā bahulīkatattā piṇḍolabhāradvājena bhikkhunā aññā byākatā:
root_text.sn48.49:3.2: ‘khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyā’ti pajānāmīti.
root_text.sn48.49:3.5: Imesaṁ kho, bhikkhave, tiṇṇannaṁ indriyānaṁ bhāvitattā bahulīkatattā piṇḍolabhāradvājena bhikkhunā aññā byākatā:
root_text.sn48.49:3.6: ‘khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyā’ti pajānāmīti.
root_text.sn48.49:3.11: ‘Jātijarāmaraṇaṁ khayan’ti kho, bhikkhave, sampassamānena piṇḍolabhāradvājena bhikkhunā aññā byākatā:
root_text.sn48.49:3.12: ‘khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyā’ti pajānāmī”ti.

translation_text.sn48.49:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Kosambī, in Ghosita’s Monastery.
translation_text.sn48.49:1.3: Now at that time Venerable Bhāradvāja the Alms-Gatherer had declared enlightenment:
translation_text.sn48.49:1.4: “I understand: ‘Rebirth is ended, the spiritual journey has been completed, what had to be done has been done, there is nothing further for this place.’”
translation_text.sn48.49:1.5: Then several mendicants went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and told him what had happened. Then they said,
translation_text.sn48.49:2.3: “What reason does Bhāradvāja the Alms-Gatherer see for doing this?”
translation_text.sn48.49:3.1: “It’s because Bhāradvāja the Alms-Gatherer has developed and cultivated three faculties that he declares enlightenment:
translation_text.sn48.49:3.2: ‘I understand: “Rebirth is ended, the spiritual journey has been completed, what had to be done has been done, there is nothing further for this place.”’
translation_text.sn48.49:3.4: The faculties of mindfulness, immersion, and wisdom.
translation_text.sn48.49:3.5: It’s because Bhāradvāja the Alms-Gatherer has developed and cultivated these three faculties that he declares enlightenment.
translation_text.sn48.49:3.7: What’s the culmination of these three faculties?
translation_text.sn48.49:3.10: Of rebirth, old age, and death.
translation_text.sn48.49:3.11: It’s because he sees that they culminate in the ending of rebirth, old age, and death that Bhāradvāja the Alms-Gatherer declares enlightenment:
translation_text.sn48.49:3.12: ‘I understand: “Rebirth is ended, the spiritual journey has been completed, what had to be done has been done, there is nothing further for this place.”’”
